transcript: Hi everyone. My name is Brian Huang and I am one of the co-founders of Glider. Glider is an early stage startup backed by a16z, Coinbase, Uniswap, and many others. So first question I have for you is, how many of you enjoy the process of opening up your wallet, connecting to an app, and manually transacting every single day throughout every day? Literally nobody here. And if you remember the first time that you onboarded to a wallet... Oops, let's see if this is working. It is not. There we go. If you remember the first time you onboarded to a wallet, this is the process that you went through. And now, this is, you know, one particular wallet, and if you've been in crypto for a while, you know that this process is actually a lot smoother than it used to be. You used to have to copy down your seed phrase and then they would challenge you to make sure that you actually copied it down. Right, and I'm not just picking on one wallet here. This is every wallet. No matter what wallet you use today, this process is at least 10 steps. And that's the big problem. Right, that's your first entry point into Web3. After that you have to fund your wallet, you have to get gas, and then you have to find applications to use, learn how to use them, and then deposit into them and track your funds. We can all agree this is never going to work for normal users. All along the way, what you've probably encountered too, is a lot of hurdles and roadblocks through this. Right, you either got to a chain you didn't have gas, you didn't know how to use an application, or you didn't know how to bridge to the particular chain that the app was built on. These are really frustrating problems. And then we have my favorite screen of all, the transaction screen. Right, so many of us have become numb to this screen. How many times have you used Jupiter or Uniswap and just scrolled to the bottom of this and clicked confirm? Right, that was never the purpose of this screen. This screen was built to provide you the assurances that you're signing the right thing. When in fact, all of us just click confirm anyways. We live in a society today where we have cars that can take us from point A to point B without any human in the loop. Nobody has to drive and it keeps you literally safe, protecting your life, from point A to point B. Why don't we have this in crypto? Why can't I go from an asset on one protocol to another asset in a different lending protocol on a different chain without having to figure out how to get there? Right, while we're in MapQuest era in crypto, the rest of the world is in the Waymo era. So what if your wallet could actually rebalance for you, move between yield sources, interact and save you from liquidation in the middle of the night? How many people could this have saved during the Terra Luna crash? Right? And the irony of all of this is we actually have truly programmable money at this point. Why aren't we using it? Every Web2 application that you see these days is a really poor copy of something that... or sorry, every Web3 application you see these days is a really poor copy of something that you can do in Web2. We're not taking full advantage of the potential that you have with onchain assets. And with that, let me introduce you to Glider. Glider is the calm, intelligent way to invest onchain. With Glider, we can automatically rebalance your assets, move you between yield sources, protect you from liquidation, react to a price trigger, buy when somebody tweets about a token. All while keeping you not having to do things with the complexities of DeFi. No gas, no signing, no bridging, and fully non-custodial. You no longer have to stare at a screen 24/7 and ensure that you, you know, don't miss a price jump in the middle of the night. Glider works for you based on your intentions. We're focused on orchestration. Now what does that mean exactly? That means how do I go from an onchain stock on one protocol to have it automatically sold when it hits a price level to sell into a stablecoin that's lending on a different protocol? Right? Today, that takes 20 different clicks, 20 different steps rather, and you have to have native gas on two different chains, maybe two different wallets. That's... that's not a way to interact onchain. Right? With Glider, we take you from point A to point B without you having to worry about how to get there. We figure out the how, you tell us the what, Glider automates that process. Now this wasn't easy to build. We built a chain agnostic system that is gas free and declarative. Right, you tell it what you want, it figures out the rest. And we did this thanks to the power of Swig, which is another Anagram company. They've helped us out to build account abstraction for Solana. And now Glider, for the last two months we've been live on EVM, on both Base and Plume, and we have over 50,000 active users. We have 30,000 portfolios today holding real value, and it's become a part of the daily ritual of our users. They come back and check their portfolios just like you would check your stock portfolio every day. Our number one goal with Glider is to grow the wealth of our users. We are here to empower people to invest without having to think about the complexities of DeFi and providing the safety and reliability that you have in your traditional investing services. And with that, let's cue the tape. I'm excited to announce, today, Glider is live on Solana. You can go to glider.fi and you can create any portfolio with any Solana-based asset. We are now one of the only apps to combine both the EVM and the SVM in a truly chain agnostic way. Come and glide with us. Thank you.

enrichment.generated_query: Glider official announcement blog technical docs Solana Breakpoint 2025

sources.title: Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform  | Drift Updates
sources.snippet:
Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform  | Drift Updates
[![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f0866da8eed4c/6867a1f64ca2c75f0d14f371_16c97ce9017b907297827c5113a068f9_Frame%201597880158.avif)](https://www.drift.trade/)
[
Launch App
](#)
## Introducing Drift v3: Built to Outperform
December 4, 2025
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6310e7dee49f08e5798eed50/693023e13e4b72ada17e0fe2_2025-12-03%2017.19.13.jpg)
[
Contents
](#)
[
Contents
](#)
[
](https://twitter.com/driftprotocol)[
](https://discord.com/invite/fMcZBH8ErM)
[
Trade Now
](https://app.drift.trade/)
![]()
![]()
Drift v3 is the protocol’s biggest performance upgrade yet. Rebuilt around trader execution, v3 delivers**10x faster fills, 10x improved liquidity, and a cleaner, more intuitive interface**than v2.
This upgrade was built for one thing: outperformance. Instant execution. Deeper liquidity. A suite of tools that helps you trade faster and with more confidence.
Drift v2 was the first to bring cross-margin and multi-asset collateral to DeFi on-chain trading. We built on Solana to deliver NASDAQ-level performance on-chain and with major Solana upgrades like Alpenglow, Firedancer and Jito’s Block Assembly Market (BAM), Drift is built to outperform as the network scales.
Since launch, Drift has surpassed 1.5B in TVL, $133B+ in total trading volume and $650M in open interest. Drift v3 is built to take those numbers and your trading even further.
**Drift v3 will be available on Dec 4, 2025.**New users will need an invite code to access Drift v3.
**DM**[**@DriftProtocol**](https://x.com/DriftProtocol)**to request access.**
## Why v3?
Drift v3 represents the protocol’s next stage of maturity: the point where years of infrastructure, liquidity, and execution improvements converge into a faster, clearer, more reliable trading experience.
**Over the past year, Drift has evolved in three critical areas:**
1. Execution speed
2. Liquidity depth
3. Trader experience## How Drift v3 outperforms
*The road to v3 was focused on improving liquidity, trading execution and speed. Here’s what that looks like in practice:*
### **Trading Execution and Speed**
* **10x faster order fills:**On Drift v3, 85% of market orders are filled within the same slot. A slot is Solana’s smallest time unit (about 400 milliseconds). On Drift, that means that trades are executed in under half a second. Instant execution.
* **15x faster take profit/stop loss triggers:**For BTC, it dropped from 15 slots (\~6 seconds) to 1 slot (\~0.4 seconds). This means your risk controls and exits now trigger immediately, reducing slippage and missed opportunities.
* **Gasless trading by default:**Unlike other DEXs, where you pay gas on every trade, Drift abstracts gas fees so that’s one less thing to worry about eating into profits
* **400ms oracle updates**: Prices update every 400 millisecond (roughly 2.5 times per second). This keeps quotes accurate and reduces the chance of stale pricing.
* **Scales with Solana:**As Solana increases block size and throughput, Drift automatically gets faster too, handling more trades per second.### **Liquidity**
* **10x reduction in slippage on market orders:**In Drift v2, large market orders could move the price by 20 bps. In v3, that’s been cut to just 2 bps. Traders now get far better pricing, especially on size.
* **5x tighter TP/SL execution:**Take-profit and stop-loss orders are far more precise. On major markets like SOL and BTC, fills are 4x tighter, while non-majors see up to 5x improvement. This means exits trigger closer to the price you set, reducing losses and protecting gains.
* **Introduction of Drift Liquidity Provider (DLP)**: Provides liquidity for both Perps and Spot markets. Community members can deposit funds into DLP to support market-making and earn a share of the pool's profits. Currently in the testing stage. Public launch in Q1 2026.
* **Expanded Market Maker Program:**With v3’s expanded market maker initiatives, long-tail altcoins now hav

tags.impact_reason: Glider addresses key UX hurdles in crypto wallets, but its actual impact on the Solana ecosystem remains to be seen.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a futuristic, autonomous glider as the semantic anchor because it directly embodies the product's name and its core value proposition: providing a smooth, 'Waymo-era' automated path through the friction-filled landscape of manual crypto transactions. The complex, tangled environment represents the '10 steps' and 'roadblocks' mentioned in the transcript. For the portrait version, I integrated the word 'INTENT' as a diegetic, holographic projection from the vehicle, which visually represents the 'intent-based' execution that powers the entire system.
visual_idea.description: A sleek, manta ray-shaped glider made of polished carbon fiber soars effortlessly through a dark, abstract canyon. The canyon walls are a chaotic tangle of glitching, red-hot data wires and blocky structures, representing manual friction. The glider itself follows a single, serene, cyan light trail, its path clear and direct. Projected from the nose of the glider is a subtle, holographic word in a clean sans-serif font: 'INTENT,' illuminating the clear path ahead. The lighting is volumetric and cinematic, with deep shadows and vibrant neon glows.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A sleek, monumental manta ray-shaped glider crafted from gleaming, highly reflective polished carbon fiber, soaring effortlessly and majestically through a deep, dark, abstract canyon. The towering canyon walls are an epic, chaotic tangle of glitching, vibrant neon red-hot data wires and imposing, blocky structures, vividly representing manual friction. The glider itself follows a single, serene, brilliant cyan light trail, its path clear and direct, cutting through the chaos. Projected from the nose of the glider is a subtle yet crisp, bright, high-contrast holographic word in a clean sans-serif font: 'INTENT,' illuminating the clear path ahead with a soft cyan glow. The entire scene is bathed in volumetric and cinematic lighting, with dramatic deep shadows contrasting sharply with vibrant neon glows from the red wires and the cyan trail and projection. Composed for a vertical 3:4 aspect ratio, emphasizing immense height, epic scale, and significant negative space at the top, making the glider feel monumental and solitary. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, cinematic volumetric lighting, extreme detail.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a futuristic, autonomous glider as the semantic anchor because it directly embodies the product's name and its core value proposition: providing a smooth, 'Waymo-era' automated path through the friction-filled landscape of manual crypto transactions. The complex, tangled environment represents the '10 steps' and 'roadblocks' mentioned in the transcript. For the portrait version, I integrated the word 'INTENT' as a diegetic, holographic projection from the vehicle, which visually represents the 'intent-based' execution that powers the entire system.
visual_idea.description: A macro close-up on the wingtip of the polished carbon fiber glider. The intricate weave of the material is in sharp focus. Blurred in the deep background, the chaotic red data streams of the environment create a strong contrast against the clean, aerodynamic lines of the vehicle. A crisp, cyan line of light is reflected perfectly along the glider's smooth, dark edge, emphasizing its frictionless nature.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A macro close-up on the wingtip of a highly polished carbon fiber glider, capturing the intricate weave of the material in ultra-sharp focus. The surface exhibits extreme reflectivity, showcasing a deep, mirror-like sheen. Blurred in the deep background, chaotic, volumetric red data streams, resembling holographic projections, create a strong, high-contrast visual against the glider's clean, aerodynamic lines. A crisp, vibrant cyan line of volumetric neon light is reflected perfectly along the glider's smooth, dark edge, dramatically emphasizing its frictionless nature. The composition is a tight, central macro frame, filling the entire square aspect ratio (1:1) with the texture and detail of the carbon fiber. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, cinematic lighting, high-contrast, shallow depth of field, studio render.

editorial.headline: a16z-Backed Glider Brings 'Waymo-Era' Portfolio Automation to Solana
editorial.standfirst: The 'financial orchestration layer' debuts on Solana to replace manual wallet friction with intent-based, gasless execution powered by Anagram's Swig.
key_takeaways: Glider launches on Solana Mainnet today, expanding its automated portfolio platform beyond its 50,000 EVM users.
The protocol is backed by tier-one capital including a16z, Coinbase Ventures, and Uniswap Labs.
Leverages Swig for Solana account abstraction, enabling gasless, chain-agnostic rebalancing without manual signing.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
Crypto user experience is stuck in the "MapQuest era"—manual, fragmented, and high-friction. Users are forced to navigate a gauntlet of 10+ steps just to execute a strategy: bridging, funding gas, approving contracts, and manually signing transactions. **Glider**, which launched on Solana today, argues that this manual transmission model is obsolete. Co-founder **Brian Huang** positions the protocol not as a wallet, but as a "financial orchestration layer"—effectively a "Waymo" for capital that moves assets from point A to point B autonomously based on user intent.

Unlike traditional wallets that require users to stare at screens to catch price jumps or avoid liquidations, Glider automates the entire lifecycle of a trade. The platform allows users to set declarative instructions—such as rebalancing assets, rotating into higher yield sources, or executing a buy order based on social sentiment—which are then executed automatically. This "set and forget" architecture aims to democratize the kind of sophisticated, 24/7 portfolio management previously reserved for institutional desks or custom bots.

The technical unlock for this automation on Solana is **Swig**, an account abstraction toolkit developed by **Anagram**. While account abstraction is gaining ground on EVM chains, it has been historically difficult to implement natively on Solana. By leveraging Swig, Glider abstracts away the complexities of gas management and signing, delivering a truly gasless and chain-agnostic experience. This allows the platform to unify EVM and SVM liquidity, enabling users to manage portfolios across **Base**, **Plume**, and now **Solana** without distinct wallets for each ecosystem.

With 50,000 active users and 30,000 portfolios already live on EVM chains, Glider’s expansion to Solana represents a stress test for high-frequency, low-latency automated DeFi. The implication for investors is significant: the ability to execute cross-chain strategies without the risk of human latency or the friction of manual bridging. By removing the "human in the loop" for execution, Glider is betting that the future of retail volume isn't in better trading terminals, but in autonomous agents that trade while you sleep.
